revision.text:
[[ar:ضريح هيه]][[arz:ضريح هيه]][[az:Hie məbədi]][[de:Hie-Schrein]][[en:Hie Shrine]][[fa:معبد هیه]][[fr:Hie-jinja]][[hu:Hie-szentély]][[it:Santuario di Hie]][[ja:日枝神社 (千代田区)]][[ko:히에 신사]][[pl:Hie-jinja]][[ru:Хиэ-дзиндзя]][[zh:日枝神社 (千代田区)]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Chiyoda, Tokyo, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hie Shrine
日枝神社 | image = Tokyo Hie-Shrine Gate.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|40|29|N|139|44|22.50|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:1000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|qid=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui no Kami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} | founded_by = {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|qid=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan|1=Ōta Dōkan}} | established = 1478 | date_destroyed = | location = 2-10-5, {{ill|Nagatachō|en|Nagatachō|qid=Q1197269|lt=Nagatachō|1=Nagatachō}}
{{ill|Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Chiyoda, Tokyo|qid=Q214051|lt=Chiyoda, Tokyo|1=Chiyoda, Tokyo}} 100-0014 | website = {{URL|http://www.hiejinja.net/jinja/english/index.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} The {{nihongo|'''Hie Shrine'''|日枝神社|Hie Jinja}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Nagatachō|en|Nagatachō|qid=Q1197269|lt=Nagatachō|1=Nagatachō}}, {{ill|Chiyoda, Tokyo|en|Chiyoda, Tokyo|qid=Q214051|lt=Chiyoda, Tokyo|1=Chiyoda, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Its June 15 {{ill|Sannō Matsuri|en|Sannō Matsuri|qid=Q218663|lt=Sannō Matsuri|1=Sannō Matsuri}} is one of the three great [[Japanese festivals]] of {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|qid=Q215646|lt=Edo|1=Edo}} (the forerunner of {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}). Other names for the shrine include Hiyoshi Sannō-sha, Hiyoshi Sannō Daigongen-sha, Edo Sannō Daigongen, Kōjimachi Sannō, Sannō-sha, and Sannō-sama. The main god of the shrine is {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|qid=Q10935105|lt=Oyamakui no Kami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. ==History== The date of establishment of the Hie Shrine is uncertain. According to one theory, {{ill|Ōta Dōkan|en|Ōta Dōkan|qid=Q386144|lt=Ōta Dōkan|1=Ōta Dōkan}} established it in 1478. Another theory identifies the Hie with the Sannō Shrine mentioned in a 1362 record of the [[Kumano Nachi Taisha]]. {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} relocated it to the grounds of {{ill|Edo Castle|en|Edo Castle|qid=Q865913|lt=Edo Castle|1=Edo Castle}}, and in 1604 his son {{ill|Tokugawa Hidetada|en|Tokugawa Hidetada|qid=Q314464|lt=Tokugawa Hidetada|1=Tokugawa Hidetada}} moved it out, so the people of {{ill|Edo|en|Edo|qid=Q215646|lt=Edo|1=Edo}} could worship there. The ''shaden'' was lost to the {{ill|Great Fire of Meireki|en|Great Fire of Meireki|qid=Q1336847|lt=Great Fire of Meireki|1=Great Fire of Meireki}} of 1657, and in 1659 {{ill|Tokugawa Ietsuna|en|Tokugawa Ietsuna|qid=Q323422|lt=Tokugawa Ietsuna|1=Tokugawa Ietsuna}} rebuilt it at its present location. The shrine stands southwest of the castle, in the ''ura kimon'' direction according to [[onmyōdō]]. From 1871 through 1946, the Hie Shrine was officially designated one of the {{Nihongo|2=官幣大社|3=Kanpei-taisha}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 126. The ''shaden'' was lost again to the {{ill|Bombing of Tokyo|en|Bombing of Tokyo|qid=Q59886|lt=Bombing of Tokyo|1=Bombing of Tokyo}} during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. The present structure dates from 1958. The Hie Shrine possesses one {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|qid=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}, a [[tachi]] (single-edged sword). It also holds 14 Important Cultural Assets, 13 swords and one {{ill|Naginata|en|Naginata|qid=Q546116|lt=Naginata|1=Naginata}}. The shrine is also one of the most popular for Japanese families to visit during the [[Shichi-Go-San]] coming-of-age festival. [[:en:Tameike-Sannō Station|Tameike-Sannō Station]] on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|en|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|qid=Q1073046|lt=Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|1=Ginza Line}} and {{ill|Tokyo Metro Namboku Line|en|Tokyo Metro Namboku Line|qid=Q520772|lt=Tokyo Metro Namboku Line|1=Tokyo Metro Namboku Line}}, {{ill|Kokkai-gijidō-mae Station|en|Kokkai-gijidō-mae Station|qid=Q1192508|lt=Kokkai-gijidō-mae Station|1=Kokkai-gijidō-mae Station}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Marunouchi Line|en|Tokyo Metro Marunouchi Line|qid=Q1147028|lt=Tokyo Metro Marunouchi Line|1=Marunouchi Line}} and {{ill|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|en|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|qid=Q1075089|lt=Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|1=Chiyoda Line}}, and {{ill|Akasaka-mitsuke Station|en|Akasaka-mitsuke Station|qid=Q602384|lt=Akasaka-mitsuke Station|1=Akasaka-mitsuke Station}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|en|Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|qid=Q1073046|lt=Tokyo Metro Ginza Line|1=Ginza Line}} and {{ill|Tokyo Metro Marunouchi Line|en|Tokyo Metro Marunouchi Line|qid=Q1147028|lt=Tokyo Metro Marunouchi Line|1=Marunouchi Line}} are the closest stops to the shrine. The shrine has escalators to help people climb up the tall hill it is located on. It also has a large series of Torii similar to those of [[Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]]{{Cite web |last=Chiyoda |first=Visit |date=2019-02-23 |title=The power spot “Hie-jinja Shrine” is a Japanese shrine full of instagrammable highlights! |url=https://visit-chiyoda.com/hie-jinja-shrine-instagram-powerspot/ |access-date=2023-11-07 |website=Visit Chiyoda |language=en-US}} ==Gallery== File:Hie Shrine top.jpg|alt=|Honden File:Hie Shrine Escalator view.jpg|alt=|Escalators from the top File:Escalator at Hie Shrine.jpg|alt=|Escalators from the top File:Hie Shrine Escalator.jpg|alt=|Escalators from the top File:Hie Shrine Big Torii with escalators in the background.jpg|alt=|View of escalators through a big torii File:Looking up the escalators at Hie Shrine.jpg|alt=|View of escalators through a big torii File:Hie Shrine big Torii.jpg|alt=|Big Torii File:Hie Shrine Torii row.jpg|alt=|Torii Row File:Hie Shrine Torii.jpg|alt=|Torii Row File:Stairs at Hie Shrine.jpg|alt= File:Hie Shrine Tokyo.jpg|alt= ==See also== *{{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (crafts: swords)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (crafts: swords)|qid=Q6591579|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (crafts: swords)|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (crafts: swords)}} ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}, Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] * ''This article incorporates material from'' [[:ja:日枝神社|日枝神社]] ''(Hie Jinja) in the Japanese Wikipedia, retrieved September 16, 2007.'' ==External links== {{Commons Category|Hie-jinja (Chiyoda, Tokyo)}} {{Hiyoshi Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|日枝神社 (千代田区)|version=103567711|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Hie Shrine]] [[ja:日枝神社 (千代田区)]] [[ja:麹町山王]] [[ar:ضريح هيه]] [[arz:ضريح هيه]] [[az:Hie məbədi]] [[de:Hie-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد هیه]] [[fr:Hie-jinja]] [[hu:Hie-szentély]] [[it:Santuario di Hie]] [[ko:히에 신사]] [[pl:Hie-jinja]] [[ru:Хиэ-дзиндзя]] [[zh:日枝神社 (千代田区)]] [[ar:ضريح هيه]] [[arz:ضريح هيه]] [[az:Hie məbədi]] [[de:Hie-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد هیه]] [[fr:Hie-jinja]] [[hu:Hie-szentély]] [[it:Santuario di Hie]] [[ko:히에 신사]] [[pl:Hie-jinja]] [[ru:Хиэ-дзиндзя]] [[zh:日枝神社 (千代田区)]] {{wikidata link|Q702042}} [[Category:1478 establishments in Asia]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Japan destroyed during World War II]] [[Category:Hie Shrine]] [[Category:Hie Shrine|!]] [[Category:Hie Shrine|&こうしまちさんのう]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Nagatachō, Tokyo]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan (Crafts)]] [[Category:National Treasures of Japan in Tokyo]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 1958]] [[Category:Religious organizations established in the 1470s]] [[Category:Sannō Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shrines in Chiyoda, Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Ten Shrines]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
